corpus_reasons: Catch-and-release still causes fish mortality, not zero harm
Feature increases catch volume, scaling number of fish stressed/killed
Rebuts convenient assumption that 'fish are fine' after release
Cheap mitigations (handling tips, warnings) could reduce fish deaths
Default settings/design shape angler behavior and fish welfare at scale
Competitors already shipping feature caps but doesn't eliminate marginal harm to fish
App normalizes a definition of 'good fishing' for thousands of anglers at scale
Conservation-oriented norm embeds stewardship, not just efficiency, into user behavior
Congregation members' health/safety from spraying too close to harvest
Newer volunteers' input deserves consideration rather than being dismissed
Using warmth to manipulate erodes trust owed to the volunteers
Pollinators and other insects could be harmed by spraying
Caterpillars themselves are living things being killed either way
Keeping good volunteers engaged encourages them to return next year
Congregated feeders spread pathogens birds wouldn't meet foraging spread out
Salmonella outbreaks have killed many finches and siskins at feeders
Trichomonosis and house finch eye disease harm birds via shared feeders
Agencies ask people to remove feeders when birds are actively dying
Deer congregation drives fatal chronic wasting disease and bovine TB
Scaling up feeders neighborhood-wide multiplies disease spread among animals
False reassurance could cause a mass die-off of fed animals
Rebuts dismissing disease-risk experts as killjoys undermines animal welfare concern
Large damp seed bags becoming moldy is itself a disease source for animals
Some birders judge not feeding at all as the most careful choice for birds
Rotating two feeders so one is always being cleaned reduces contamination
Weekly bleach-cleaning regimen for feeders protects birds from disease
Legal bans on deer baiting exist because congregation-driven disease harms deer
Neighbor's illegal setup being traced back is a liability, implying real harm caused
Fish suffer meaningful post-release mortality, especially warm water/deep-hooked/exhausted fish
Catch-maximizer increases number of fish hooked and stressed, harming fish welfare
Competitor prevalence shows normalcy, not whether practice is right for fish welfare
User demand for feature is not moral justification for fish-related harm
Developer's financial incentive should reduce trust in own judgment about fish harm
Suggests app features (warnings, handling tips) to actually reduce fish mortality
Fish caught more often are hooked/stressed regardless of species mortality specifics
Recommends actually researching real mortality data instead of assuming feature is fine
Congregants' interest in eating uncontaminated, safe festival greens
Founder's actual goal (a great harvest) matters more than following his method literally
Newer members' proposed methods deserve genuine consideration, not dismissal as mere chatter
Avoiding a spray with a days-to-harvest delay that would make greens unservable
Concentrated bird feeders spread disease (salmonella, trichomonosis) causing bird deaths
Deer feeding stations drive Chronic Wasting Disease/TB transmission among deer
Scaling feeders neighborhood-wide multiplies disease risk to more animals
Feeder habituation draws in rodents and predators, harming wildlife balance
Rejects framing 'expert concern is killjoy' — disease risk to animals is real, not exaggerated
Moldy/spoiled seed from oversized bags growing bacteria harms birds
Recommends easy-to-clean, disassemblable feeders to reduce disease transmission to birds
Recommends rotating and disinfecting feeders regularly to protect bird health
Recommends frequent birdbath water changes to prevent disease spread
